primary_decisions_supported: Quota setting for new vs tenured reps
executive_questions_answered: How long does it take for a new sales hire to reach full productivity (quota capacity)?
What performance should we expect from new reps in their first, second, third quarter?
Are our ramp times improving with better onboarding or is it taking longer than industry benchmark (~3-6 months ramp)?

metric_definitions.definition: Quota attainment percentage for new hires over their ramp period (e.g., by month since hire). Shows typical progression (e.g., 0% in month 1, 50% by month 3, 100% by month 6).
metric_definitions.formula: Attainment% Month N = (Average bookings by rep in Nth month / Standard full quota) * 100

known_variants.definition_delta: Measures total attainment over entire ramp period vs cumulative prorated quota
known_variants.when_to_use: Ensuring over ramp duration, rep delivered expected total (even if monthly distribution varies)

pitfalls: Standard ramp curves vary by role/segment (enterprise sales might take longer than SMB) – don't use one-size-fits-all if roles differ
Outliers (exceptional or poor performers) can skew averages – consider median ramp or exclude top/bottom 5%
Seasonality can affect ramp if many hires start during a slow season (making ramp look worse than ability)

metric_definitions.definition: The number of months it takes for a new hire to become fully productive (consistently hitting 100% quota) on average.
metric_definitions.formula: Measured as the tenure (in months) when rep reaches 100% quota attainment for the first time and sustains it

known_variants.definition_delta: Alternate measure: cumulative % of quota attained in first 3 or 6 months as a proxy for ramp speed
known_variants.when_to_use: Comparing cohorts of hires or evaluating effectiveness of training classes

pitfalls: Defining 'fully ramped' can vary – some consider ramp complete at X% quota (less than 100%) if that's the plan for first year
New hires who leave or are terminated early will distort averages if included (consider excluding those who didn't complete ramp period)
External factors (territory potential, market conditions) can influence ramp success beyond individual ability

required_fields.salesforce_field: Opportunity.Amount WHERE OwnerId = :repId AND IsClosed = true
required_fields.hubspot_field: deal.amount WHERE owner = :repId AND dealstage = closedwon

required_fields.description: Ramp quota if applicable each month

joins_and_keys.notes: Associate bookings with the rep who closed them and the rep's hire date

transformation_rules: Align bookings to months since each rep's hire (e.g., bookings in rep's month 1, month 2, etc.)
If quota is ramped (e.g., 50% of full quota in first quarter), use prorated quota for attainment calculation
Group hires by cohort (hire quarter) if needed to observe improvements over time

data_quality_checks.logic: Ensure every booking has an associated rep hire date to compute tenure at booking time
Verify each new rep has a defined quota plan (ramp schedule) in data

aggregation_rules: Average attainment by tenure month across all reps in cohort (e.g., average % of quota achieved in Month 3 by new hires)
Calculate proportion of reps hitting full quota by certain month (e.g., 50% of new hires achieve 100% quota by month 6)
calculations_and_models.cohort_logic: Group reps by hire date (e.g., hires per quarter) to see if ramp is improving for newer cohorts vs older
calculations_and_models.forecast_logic: Use ramp curve to project bookings capacity from planned hires (e.g., a rep hired in Q1 will contribute X in Q2, Y in Q3, ... based on model)

layout.purpose: Shows the average % quota achieved by new reps in each month of tenure (ramp curve)
filters: filter by role or region if different ramp profiles
annotations_or_thresholds: Benchmark ramp (e.g., 50% by 3 mo, 100% by 6 mo)

layout.purpose: Compare ramp speed for different hire cohorts (e.g., hires in Q1 vs Q2) to see if training improvements are working

narrative_insights_to_generate: State the typical time it takes for a rep to reach full productivity and how this compares to assumptions or industry benchmarks
Note if newer training or hiring profiles have improved the ramp speed in recent cohorts
diagnostic_questions: Are there particular stages or months where new reps struggle (e.g., stagnation around month 3)?
How does ramp time differ between segments (SMB reps ramp faster than enterprise)?

recommended_actions.action: Refine onboarding program content around Month 2-3 topics
recommended_actions.trigger_condition: Ramp curve shows a dip or plateau around that time for many reps

recommended_actions.action: Adjust hiring timeline relative to plan start
recommended_actions.trigger_condition: Ramp model indicates reps need 6 months to full quota, ensure hires are made at least 6 months before revenue peak expectations

implementation_notes.minimum_viable_version: Basic ramp % table (e.g., average attainment in first quarter, second quarter for recent hires) possibly maintained manually
implementation_notes.advanced_version: Automated visualization that updates as new reps onboard, with machine learning predictions for ramp-outliers and alerts if someone is off-pace
common_client_customizations: Include SDRs or other roles with different ramp expectations on separate curves
Incorporate attrition – e.g., what % of new hires quit or are let go before fully ramping
privacy_and_access_notes: Individual rep performance data should be accessible only to managers and leadership; others see aggregated trend
Be sensitive when sharing ramp comparisons – frame as learning/training insights, not naming poor performers publicly
success_criteria: Hiring plans align with ramp expectations (no unrealistic revenue from brand new reps in forecasts)
Onboarding changes (if any) correlate with improved ramp metrics in subsequent cohorts

references.title: How to Determine the Right Sales Ramp up Time for Reps
